title: Why the second round of France's municipal elections matters
subhed: The vote on Sunday, March 22, will provide valuable national insights, with a focus on the far-right's result, the impact of mergers or divisions within the left and right and the right's gamble to stay in the race.

title: Iranian missiles sow panic in Israeli town
Trump settles scores with former prosecutor Robert Mueller following news of his death
India faces major gas shortage as Middle East conflict disrupts supply
subhed: With restrictions, a black market and illegal storage networks, India's vulnerability has been exposed by the conflict affecting the Strait of Hormuz, through which 90% of its liquefied petroleum gas transits.

title: Cuba hit by second nationwide blackout in a week
subhed: Cuba's national power network failed for the second time in less than a week, leaving streets pitch black and forcing people to navigate using flashlights. Blackouts have intensified because of aging infrastructure and a US oil blockade.

title: London exhibit highlights a meeting between two major suffragettes
subhed: The Florence Nightingale Museum, named after the nurse who elevated her profession and founded the first school dedicated to nursing, is hosting an exhibition devoted to the famous suffragette Emmeline Pankhurst.

title: Dnipro, the 'heart of Ukraine' where residents, refugees and military live side by side
subhed: This industrial city, considered a stronghold of the Jewish community, embodies the country's history and a certain idea of resistance to the advances of the Russian army, four years after the start of the war.

subhed: Since stripping Senegal of the continental championship title, ruling the team had 'forfeited,' the Confédération Africaine de Football (CAF) has faced intense criticism over its ability to manage competitions.

title: In Morocco's Agafay Desert, the Kasbah d'If combines luxury, serenity and vast open spaces
subhed: Located about 30 kilometers from Marrakech, the Kasbah d'If offers a stunning panorama of the Atlas Mountains. Built using adobe, this citadel-like 5-star hotel showcases Moroccan craftsmanship and a focus on well-being.

title: Soaring ammunition cost turns Iran conflict into 'war of stockpiles'
subhed: The daily interception of thousands of missiles and drones launched by Tehran over the past three weeks has put severe strain on already limited reserves. The situation is particularly critical for France, which is bound by defense agreements with several Middle Eastern countries.
